001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server;
018
019import static java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.*;
020import static java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.HttpPartType.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
025
026import java.io.*;
027import java.nio.charset.*;
028import java.time.*;
029import java.util.*;
030import java.util.regex.*;
031
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.http.header.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.httppart.bean.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.httppart.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.logger.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.sse.*;
048import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.util.*;
049
050import jakarta.servlet.*;
051import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
052
053/**
054 * Represents an HTTP response for a REST resource.
055 *
056 * <p>
057 *    The {@link RestResponse} object is an extension of the <l>HttpServletResponse</l> class
058 *    with various built-in convenience methods for use in building REST interfaces.
059 *    It can be accessed by passing it as a parameter on your REST Java method:
060 * </p>
061 *
062 * <p class='bjava'>
063 *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(...)
064 *    <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod(RestResponse <jv>res</jv>) {...}
065 * </p>
066 *
067 * <p>
068 *    The primary methods on this class are:
069 * </p>
070 * <ul class='javatree'>
071 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestResponse}
072 *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
073 *       <li>Methods for setting response headers:
074 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
075 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#addHeader(HttpHeader) addHeader(HttpHeader)}
076 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#addHeader(String,String) addHeader(String,String)}
077 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#containsHeader(String) containsHeader(String)}
078 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getHeader(String) getHeader(String)}
079 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setCharacterEncoding(String) setCharacterEncoding(String)}
080 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setContentType(String) setContentType(String)}
081 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setHeader(HttpHeader) setHeader(HttpHeader)}
082 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setHeader(HttpPartSchema,String,Object) setHeader(HttpPartSchema,String,Object)}
083 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setHeader(String,Object) setHeader(String,Object)}
084 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setHeader(String,String) setHeader(String,String)}
085 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setMaxHeaderLength(int) setMaxHeaderLength(int)}
086 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setSafeHeaders() setSafeHeaders()}
087 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#downloadAs(String) downloadAs(String)}
088 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#eTag(String) eTag(String)}
089 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#eTag(EntityTag) eTag(EntityTag)}
090 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#lastModified(Instant) lastModified(Instant)}
091 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#lastModified(ZonedDateTime) lastModified(ZonedDateTime)}
092 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#cacheControl(String) cacheControl(String)}
093 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#cacheControl(CacheControl.Builder) cacheControl(CacheControl.Builder)}
094 *       </ul>
095 *       <li>Methods for setting response bodies:
096 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
097 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#flushBuffer() flushBuffer()}
098 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getDirectWriter(String) getDirectWriter(String)}
099 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getNegotiatedOutputStream() getNegotiatedOutputStream()}
100 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getNegotiatedWriter() getNegotiatedWriter()}
101 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getSerializerMatch() getSerializerMatch()}
102 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getWriter() getWriter()}
103 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#sendPlainText(String) sendPlainText(String)}
104 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#sendRedirect(String) sendRedirect(String)}
105 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setContentSchema(HttpPartSchema) setContentSchema(HttpPartSchema)}
106 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setContent(Object) setOutput(Object)}
107 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setSerializer(Serializer) setSerializer(Serializer)}
108 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setResponseBeanMeta(ResponseBeanMeta) setResponseBeanMeta(ResponseBeanMeta)}
109 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setException(Throwable) setException(Throwable)}
110 *       </ul>
111 *       <li>Other:
112 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
113 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getAttributes() getAttributes()}
114 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getContext() getContext()}
115 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#getOpContext() getOpContext()}
116 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setAttribute(String,Object) setAttribute(String,Object)}
117 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setDebug() setDebug()}
118 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setNoTrace() setNoTrace()}
119 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#setStatus(int) setStatus(int)}
120 *       </ul>
121 *    </ul>
122 * </ul>
123 *
124 */
125@SuppressWarnings({
126   "resource", // HttpServletResponseWrapper is managed by servlet container
127   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., HEADER_ContentType)
128})
129public class RestResponse extends HttpServletResponseWrapper {
130
131   private static final String HEADER_ContentType = "Content-Type";
132   private static final String ARG_VALUE = "value";
133   private static final Pattern ENCODING_DISABLED_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("(identity|\\*)\\s*;\\s*q\\s*=\\s*(0(?!\\.)|0\\.0)");
134
135   private HttpServletResponse inner;
136   private final RestRequest request;
137
138   private Optional<Object> content;  // The POJO being sent to the output.
139   private ServletOutputStream sos;
140   private FinishableServletOutputStream os;
141   private FinishablePrintWriter w;
142   private ResponseBeanMeta responseBeanMeta;
143   private RestOpContext opContext;
144   private Optional<HttpPartSchema> contentSchema;
145   private Serializer serializer;
146   private Optional<SerializerMatch> serializerMatch;
147   private boolean safeHeaders;
148   private int maxHeaderLength = 8096;
149
150   /**
151    * Constructor.
152    */
153   @SuppressWarnings({
154      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for response initialization
155      "java:S112"   // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
156   })
157   RestResponse(RestOpContext opContext, RestSession session, RestRequest req) throws Exception {
158      super(session.getResponse());
159
160      inner = session.getResponse();
161      request = req;
162
163      this.opContext = opContext;
164      responseBeanMeta = opContext.getResponseMeta();
165
166      var context = session.getContext();
167
168      try {
169         var passThroughHeaders = request.getHeaderParam("x-response-headers").orElse(null);
170         if (nn(passThroughHeaders)) {
171            var m = context.getPartParser().getPartSession().parse(HEADER, null, passThroughHeaders, MarshallingContext.DEFAULT.getClassMeta(JsonMap.class));
172            for (var e : m.entrySet())
173               addHeader(e.getKey(), resolveUris(e.getValue()));
174         }
175      } catch (Exception e1) {
176         throw new BadRequest(e1, "Invalid format for header 'x-response-headers'.  Must be in URL-encoded format.");
177      }
178
179      // Find acceptable charset
180      var h = request.getHeaderParam("accept-charset").orElse(null);
181      Charset charset = null;
182      if (h == null)
183         charset = opContext.getDefaultCharset();
184      else
185         for (var r : StringRanges.of(h).toList()) {
186            if (r.getQValue() > 0) {
187               if (r.getName().equals("*"))
188                  charset = opContext.getDefaultCharset();
189               else if (Charset.isSupported(r.getName()))
190                  charset = Charset.forName(r.getName());
191               if (nn(charset))
192                  break;
193            }
194         }
195
196      request.getContext().getDefaultResponseHeaders().forEach(x -> addHeader(x.getName(), resolveCspNonce(maybeResolveUris(x.getName(), x.getValue()))));  // Done this way to avoid list/array copy.
197
198      opContext.getDefaultResponseHeaders().forEach(x -> addHeader(x.getName(), resolveCspNonce(maybeResolveUris(x.getName(), x.getValue()))));
199
200      if (charset == null)
201         throw new NotAcceptable("No supported charsets in header ''Accept-Charset'': ''{0}''", request.getHeaderParam("Accept-Charset").orElse(null));
202      inner.setCharacterEncoding(charset.name());
203
204   }
205
206   /**
207    * Adds a response header.
208    *
209    * <p>
210    * Any previous header values are preserved.
211    *
212    * <p>
213    * Value is added at the end of the headers.
214    *
215    * <p>
216    * If the header is a {@link HttpUriHeader}, the URI will be resolved using the {@link RestRequest#getUriResolver()} object.
217    *
218    * <p>
219    * Note that per <a class='doclink' href='https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec4.html#sec4.2'>RFC2616</a>,
220    * only headers defined as comma-delimited lists [i.e., #(values)] should be defined as multiple message header fields.
221    *
222    * @param header The header.
223    * @return This object.
224    */
225   public RestResponse addHeader(HttpHeader header) {
226      if (header == null) {
227         // Do nothing.
228      } else if (header instanceof HttpUriHeader header2) {
229         addHeader(header2.getName(), resolveUris(header2.getValue()));
230      } else {
231         addHeader(header.getName(), header.getValue());
232      }
233      return this;
234   }
235
236   /**
237    * Adds a response header with the given name and value.
238    *
239    * <p>
240    * This method allows response headers to have multiple values.
241    *
242    * <p>
243    * A no-op of either the name or value is <jk>null</jk>.
244    *
245    * <p>
246    * Note that per <a class='doclink' href='https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec4.html#sec4.2'>RFC2616</a>,
247    * only headers defined as comma-delimited lists [i.e., #(values)] should be defined as multiple message header fields.
248    *
249    * @param name The header name.
250    * @param value The header value.
251    */
252   @Override
253   public void addHeader(String name, String value) {
254      if (nn(name) && nn(value)) {
255         if (eqic(name, HEADER_ContentType))
256            setHeader(name, value);
257         else {
258            if (safeHeaders)
259               value = stripInvalidHttpHeaderChars(value);
260            value = abbreviate(value, maxHeaderLength);
261            inner.addHeader(name, value);
262         }
263      }
264   }
265
266   /**
267    * Forces any content in the buffer to be written to the client.
268    *
269    * <p>
270    * A call to this method automatically commits the response, meaning the status code and headers will be written.
271    *
272    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurred.
273    */
274   @Override
275   public void flushBuffer() throws IOException {
276      if (nn(w))
277         w.flush();
278      if (nn(os))
279         os.flush();
280      inner.flushBuffer();
281   }
282
283   /**
284    * Shortcut for calling <c>getRequest().getAttributes()</c>.
285    *
286    * @return The request attributes object.
287    */
288   public RequestAttributes getAttributes() { return request.getAttributes(); }
289
290   /**
291    * Wrapper around {@link #getCharacterEncoding()} that converts the value to a {@link Charset}.
292    *
293    * @return The request character encoding converted to a {@link Charset}.
294    */
295   public Charset getCharset() {
296      var s = getCharacterEncoding();
297      return s == null ? null : Charset.forName(s);
298   }
299
300   /**
301    * Returns the output that was set by calling {@link #setContent(Object)}.
302    *
303    * <p>
304    * If it's null, then {@link #setContent(Object)} wasn't called.
305    * <br>If it contains an empty, then <c>setObject(<jk>null</jk>)</c> was called.
306    * <br>Otherwise, {@link #setContent(Object)} was called with a non-null value.
307    *
308    * @return The output object, or <jk>null</jk> if {@link #setContent(Object)} was never called.
309    */
310   public Optional<Object> getContent() { return content; }
311
312   /**
313    * Returns this value cast to the specified class.
314    *
315    * @param <T> The class to cast to.
316    * @param c The class to cast to.
317    * @return This value cast to the specified class, or <jk>null</jk> if the object doesn't exist or isn't the specified type.
318    */
319   @SuppressWarnings({
320      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked cast
321   })
322   public <T> T getContent(Class<T> c) {
323      if (isContentOfType(c))
324         return (T)getRawOutput();
325      return null;
326   }
327
328   /**
329    * Returns the schema of the response content.
330    *
331    * @return The schema of the response content, never <jk>null</jk>.
332    */
333   public Optional<HttpPartSchema> getContentSchema() {
334      if (nn(contentSchema))
335         return contentSchema;
336      if (nn(responseBeanMeta))
337         contentSchema = opt(responseBeanMeta.getSchema());
338      else {
339         var rbm = opContext.getResponseBeanMeta(getContent(Object.class));
340         if (nn(rbm))
341            contentSchema = opt(rbm.getSchema());
342         else
343            contentSchema = opte();
344      }
345      return contentSchema;
346   }
347
348   /**
349    * Returns access to the inner {@link RestContext} of the class of this method.
350    *
351    * @return The {@link RestContext} of this class.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
352    */
353   public RestContext getContext() { return request.getContext(); }
354
355   /**
356    * Convenience method meant to be used when rendering directly to a browser with no buffering.
357    *
358    * <p>
359    * Sets the header <js>"x-content-type-options=nosniff"</js> so that output is rendered immediately on IE and Chrome
360    * without any buffering for content-type sniffing.
361    *
362    * <p>
363    * This can be useful if you want to render a streaming 'console' on a web page.
364    *
365    * @param contentType The value to set as the <c>Content-Type</c> on the response.
366    * @return The raw writer.
367    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
368    */
369   public PrintWriter getDirectWriter(String contentType) throws IOException {
370      setContentType(contentType);
371      setHeader("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff");
372      setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
373      return getWriter(true, true);
374   }
375
376   /**
377    * Returns the wrapped servlet request.
378    *
379    * @return The wrapped servlet request.
380    */
381   public HttpServletResponse getHttpServletResponse() { return inner; }
382
383   /**
384    * Returns the <c>Content-Type</c> header stripped of the charset attribute if present.
385    *
386    * @return The <c>media-type</c> portion of the <c>Content-Type</c> header.
387    */
388   public MediaType getMediaType() { return MediaType.of(getContentType()); }
389
390   /**
391    * Equivalent to {@link HttpServletResponse#getOutputStream()}, except wraps the output stream if an {@link Encoder}
392    * was found that matched the <c>Accept-Encoding</c> header.
393    *
394    * @return A negotiated output stream.
395    * @throws NotAcceptable If unsupported Accept-Encoding value specified.
396    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
397    */
398   @SuppressWarnings({
399      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for content negotiation
400   })
401   public FinishableServletOutputStream getNegotiatedOutputStream() throws NotAcceptable, IOException {
402      if (os == null) {
403         Encoder encoder = null;
404         var encoders = request.getOpContext().getEncoders();
405
406         var ae = request.getHeaderParam("Accept-Encoding").orElse(null);
407         if (ne(ae)) {
408            var match = encoders.getEncoderMatch(ae);
409            if (match == null) {
410               // Identity should always match unless "identity;q=0" or "*;q=0" is specified.
411               if (ENCODING_DISABLED_PATTERN.matcher(ae).find()) {
412                  throw new NotAcceptable("Unsupported encoding in request header ''Accept-Encoding'': ''{0}''\n\tSupported codings: {1}", ae, Json5.DEFAULT.of(encoders.getSupportedEncodings()));
413               }
414            } else {
415               encoder = match.getEncoder();
416               var encoding = match.getEncoding();
417
418               // Some clients don't recognize identity as an encoding, so don't set it.
419               if (! encoding.equals("identity"))
420                  setHeader("content-encoding", encoding);
421            }
422         }
423         var sos2 = getOutputStream();
424         os = new FinishableServletOutputStream(encoder == null ? sos2 : encoder.getOutputStream(sos2));
425      }
426      return os;
427   }
428
429   /**
430    * Equivalent to {@link HttpServletResponse#getWriter()}, except wraps the output stream if an {@link Encoder} was
431    * found that matched the <c>Accept-Encoding</c> header and sets the <c>Content-Encoding</c>
432    * header to the appropriate value.
433    *
434    * @return The negotiated writer.
435    * @throws NotAcceptable If unsupported charset in request header Accept-Charset.
436    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
437    */
438   public FinishablePrintWriter getNegotiatedWriter() throws NotAcceptable, IOException { return getWriter(false, false); }
439
440   /**
441    * Returns access to the inner {@link RestOpContext} of this method.
442    *
443    * @return The {@link RestOpContext} of this method.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
444    */
445   public RestOpContext getOpContext() { return request.getOpContext(); }
446
447   /**
448    * Returns a ServletOutputStream suitable for writing binary data in the response.
449    *
450    * <p>
451    * The servlet container does not encode the binary data.
452    *
453    * <p>
454    * Calling <c>flush()</c> on the ServletOutputStream commits the response.
455    * Either this method or <c>getWriter</c> may be called to write the content, not both, except when reset has been called.
456    *
457    * @return The stream.
458    * @throws IOException If stream could not be accessed.
459    */
460   @Override
461   public ServletOutputStream getOutputStream() throws IOException {
462      if (sos == null)
463         sos = inner.getOutputStream();
464      return sos;
465   }
466
467   /**
468    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if {@link #getOutputStream()} has been called.
469    *
470    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@link #getOutputStream()} has been called.
471    */
472   public boolean getOutputStreamCalled() { return nn(sos); }
473
474   /**
475    * Returns the metadata about this response.
476    *
477    * @return
478    *    The metadata about this response.
479    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
480    */
481   public ResponseBeanMeta getResponseBeanMeta() { return responseBeanMeta; }
482
483   /**
484    * Returns the matching serializer and media type for this response.
485    *
486    * @return The matching serializer, never <jk>null</jk>.
487    */
488   public Optional<SerializerMatch> getSerializerMatch() {
489      if (nn(serializerMatch))
490         return serializerMatch;
491      if (nn(serializer)) {
492         serializerMatch = opt(new SerializerMatch(getMediaType(), serializer));
493      } else {
494         serializerMatch = opContext.getSerializers().getSerializerMatch(request.getHeaderParam("Accept").orElse("*/*"));
495      }
496      return serializerMatch;
497   }
498
499   /**
500    * Returns the writer to the response content.
501    *
502    * <p>
503    * This methods bypasses any specified encoders and returns a regular unbuffered writer.
504    * Use the {@link #getNegotiatedWriter()} method if you want to use the matched encoder (if any).
505    *
506    * @return The writer.
507    * @throws IOException If writer could not be accessed.
508    */
509   @Override
510   public PrintWriter getWriter() throws IOException { return getWriter(true, false); }
511
512   /**
513    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the response contains output.
514    *
515    * <p>
516    * This implies {@link #setContent(Object)} has been called on this object.
517    *
518    * <p>
519    * Note that this also returns <jk>true</jk> even if {@link #setContent(Object)} was called with a <jk>null</jk>
520    * value as this means the response contains an output value of <jk>null</jk> as opposed to no value at all.
521    *
522    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the response contains output.
523    */
524   public boolean hasContent() {
525      return nn(content);
526   }
527
528   /**
529    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this response object is of the specified type.
530    *
531    * @param c The type to check against.
532    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this response object is of the specified type.
533    */
534   public boolean isContentOfType(Class<?> c) {
535      return c.isInstance(getRawOutput());
536   }
537
538   /**
539    * Sets the output to a plain-text message regardless of the content type.
540    *
541    * @param text The output text to send.
542    * @return This object.
543    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to write to the writer.
544    */
545   public RestResponse sendPlainText(String text) throws IOException {
546      setContentType("text/plain");
547      getNegotiatedWriter().write(text);
548      return this;
549   }
550
551   /**
552    * Creates a fluent Server-Sent Events helper for this response.
553    *
554    * @return The SSE response helper.
555    * @throws IOException If the writer could not be created.
556    */
557   public SseResponseSupport sse() throws IOException {
558      return new SseResponseSupport(this);
559   }
560
561   /**
562    * Redirects to the specified URI.
563    *
564    * <p>
565    * Relative URIs are always interpreted as relative to the context root.
566    * This is similar to how WAS handles redirect requests, and is different from how Tomcat handles redirect requests.
567    *
568    * @param uri The redirection URL.
569    * @throws IOException If an input or output exception occurs
570    */
571   @Override
572   public void sendRedirect(String uri) throws IOException {
573      var c = (!uri.isEmpty() ? uri.charAt(0) : 0);
574      if (c != '/' && uri.indexOf("://") == -1)
575         uri = request.getContextPath() + '/' + uri;
576      inner.sendRedirect(uri);
577   }
578
579   /**
580    * Shortcut for calling <c>getRequest().setAttribute(String,Object)</c>.
581    *
582    * @param name The property name.
583    * @param value The property value.
584    * @return This object.
585    */
586   public RestResponse setAttribute(String name, Object value) {
587      request.setAttribute(name, value);
588      return this;
589   }
590
591   /**
592    * Sets the HTTP output on the response.
593    *
594    * <p>
595    * The object type can be anything allowed by the registered response handlers.
596    *
597    * <p>
598    * Calling this method is functionally equivalent to returning the object in the REST Java method.
599    *
600    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
601    * <p class='bjava'>
602    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/example2/{personId}"</js>)
603    *    <jk>public void</jk> doGet(RestResponse <jv>res</jv>, <ja>@Path</ja> UUID <jv>personId</jv>) {
604    *       Person <jv>person</jv> = getPersonById(<jv>personId</jv>);
605    *       <jv>res</jv>.setOutput(<jv>person</jv>);
606    *    }
607    * </p>
608    *
609    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
610    *    <li class='note'>
611    *       Calling this method with a <jk>null</jk> value is NOT the same as not calling this method at all.
612    *       <br>A <jk>null</jk> output value means we want to serialize <jk>null</jk> as a response (e.g. as a JSON <c>null</c>).
613    *       <br>Not calling this method or returning a value means you're handing the response yourself via the underlying stream or writer.
614    * </ul>
615    *
616    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
617    *    <li class='ja'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Rest#responseProcessors()}
618    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestOpAnnotatedMethodBasics">@RestOp-Annotated Method Basics</a>
619    * </ul>
620    *
621    * @param output The output to serialize to the connection.
622    * @return This object.
623    */
624   public RestResponse setContent(Object output) {
625      this.content = opt(output);
626      return this;
627   }
628
629   /**
630    * Specifies the schema for the response content.
631    *
632    * <p>
633    * Used by schema-aware serializers such as {@link OpenApiSerializer}.  Ignored by other serializers.
634    *
635    * @param schema The content schema
636    * @return This object.
637    */
638   public RestResponse setContentSchema(HttpPartSchema schema) {
639      this.contentSchema = opt(schema);
640      return this;
641   }
642
643   /**
644    * Forces the {@link Serializer} used to serialize the response body set via {@link #setContent(Object)} (or an
645    * equivalent return from the REST method), instead of choosing one from the <c>Accept</c> header.
646    *
647    * <p>
648    * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.SerializedPojoProcessor} uses {@link #getSerializerMatch()}, which
649    * returns a {@link SerializerMatch} built from this serializer when non-<jk>null</jk>.
650    *
651    * <p>
652    * <c>Content-Type</c> is still applied by the response processor: it prefers {@link #getMediaType()} if set, then
653    * the match media type, then {@link Serializer#getResponseContentType()}. Call {@link #setContentType(String)} (or
654    * another header setter) if you need a specific type before serialization runs.
655    *
656    * @param value The serializer to use. Can be <jk>null</jk> to clear the override and use normal <c>Accept</c>
657    *    negotiation on the next {@link #getSerializerMatch()} call.
658    * @return This object.
659    */
660   @SuppressWarnings({
661      "java:S2789" // Null invalidates lazy Optional cache so getSerializerMatch() recomputes next access.
662   })
663   public RestResponse setSerializer(Serializer value) {
664      serializer = value;
665      serializerMatch = null;
666      return this;
667   }
668
669   /**
670    * Shortcut for calling <c>setDebug(<jk>true</jk>)</c>.
671    *
672    * @return This object.
673    * @throws IOException If bodies could not be cached.
674    */
675   public RestResponse setDebug() throws IOException {
676      return setDebug(true);
677   }
678
679   /**
680    * Sets the <js>"Debug"</js> attribute to the specified boolean.
681    *
682    * <p>
683    * This flag is used by {@link CallLogger} to help determine how a request should be logged.
684    *
685    * @param b The attribute value.
686    * @return This object.
687    * @throws IOException If bodies could not be cached.
688    */
689   public RestResponse setDebug(Boolean b) throws IOException {
690      request.setDebug(b);
691      if (isTrue(b))
692         inner = CachingHttpServletResponse.wrap(inner);
693      return this;
694   }
695
696   /**
697    * Sets the <js>"Exception"</js> attribute to the specified throwable.
698    *
699    * <p>
700    * This exception is used by {@link CallLogger} for logging purposes.
701    *
702    * @param t The attribute value.
703    * @return This object.
704    */
705   public RestResponse setException(Throwable t) {
706      request.setException(t);
707      return this;
708   }
709
710   /**
711    * Sets a response header.
712    *
713    * <p>
714    * Any previous header values are removed.
715    *
716    * <p>
717    * Value is added at the end of the headers.
718    *
719    * @param header The header.
720    * @return This object.
721    */
722   public RestResponse setHeader(HttpHeader header) {
723      if (header == null) {
724         // Do nothing.
725      } else if (header instanceof HttpUriHeader header2) {
726         setHeader(header2.getName(), resolveUris(header2.getValue()));
727      } else {
728         var v = header.getValue();
729         if (nn(v) && v.indexOf("://") != -1)
730            v = resolveUris(v);
731         setHeader(header.getName(), v);
732      }
733      return this;
734   }
735
736   /**
737    * Sets <l>Content-Disposition</l> to <js>attachment</js> so browsers typically download the body, using the given
738    * suggested file name (quoted {@code filename} parameter).
739    *
740    * <p>
741    * Shorthand for {@link #setHeader(HttpHeader) setHeader}({@link ContentDisposition#attachment(String)
742    * ContentDisposition.attachment(filename)}).
743    * </p>
744    *
745    * <h5 class='figure'>Example</h5>
746    * <p class='bjava'>
747    *    <jv>res</jv>.downloadAs(<js>"example.pdf"</js>).setContent(<jv>pdfBytes</jv>);
748    * </p>
749    *
750    * @param filename Suggested download name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>, blank, or contain CR/LF.
751    * @return This object.
752    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code filename} is invalid.
753    * @see ContentDisposition#attachment(String)
754    */
755   public RestResponse downloadAs(String filename) {
756      return setHeader(ContentDisposition.attachment(filename));
757   }
758
759   /**
760    * Sets the <c>ETag</c> response header to the supplied wire value.
761    *
762    * <p>
763    * The value is validated through {@link EntityTag#of(Object)} — strong tags must be quoted
764    * (<c>"v1"</c>), weak tags must be prefixed with <c>W/</c> (<c>W/"v1"</c>), and the wildcard
765    * <c>*</c> is accepted. The header is serialized in canonical form (re-quoted for strong tags,
766    * <c>W/</c>-prefixed for weak tags).
767    *
768    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
769    * <p class='bjava'>
770    *    <jv>res</jv>.eTag(<js>"\"v42\""</js>);  <jc>// ETag: "v42"</jc>
771    *    <jv>res</jv>.eTag(<js>"W/\"v42\""</js>);  <jc>// ETag: W/"v42"</jc>
772    * </p>
773    *
774    * @param value The entity-tag wire value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>; must match the
775    *    {@code entity-tag} grammar per RFC 7232 §2.3.
776    * @return This object.
777    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is malformed.
778    * @see RestRequest#checkPreconditions(RestResponse)
779    */
780   public RestResponse eTag(String value) {
781      return eTag(EntityTag.of(value));
782   }
783
784   /**
785    * Sets the <c>ETag</c> response header to the canonical wire form of the supplied {@link EntityTag}.
786    *
787    * @param value The entity tag. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
788    * @return This object.
789    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
790    */
791   public RestResponse eTag(EntityTag value) {
792      assertArgNotNull(ARG_VALUE, value);
793      return setHeader(ETag.of(value.toString()));
794   }
795
796   /**
797    * Sets the <c>Last-Modified</c> response header to the supplied instant formatted as an
798    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7231#section-7.1.1.1'>RFC 7231 IMF-fixdate</a>.
799    *
800    * <p>
801    * The instant is rendered in GMT regardless of the JVM's default timezone. The fractional-second
802    * portion is truncated, matching the per-spec one-second resolution of HTTP-date timestamps.
803    *
804    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
805    * <p class='bjava'>
806    *    <jv>res</jv>.lastModified(Instant.<jsm>parse</jsm>(<js>"2026-05-22T00:00:00Z"</js>));
807    *    <jc>// Last-Modified: Fri, 22 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT</jc>
808    * </p>
809    *
810    * @param value The last-modified timestamp. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
811    * @return This object.
812    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
813    * @see RestRequest#checkPreconditions(RestResponse)
814    */
815   public RestResponse lastModified(Instant value) {
816      assertArgNotNull(ARG_VALUE, value);
817      return lastModified(value.atZone(ZoneOffset.UTC));
818   }
819
820   /**
821    * Sets the <c>Last-Modified</c> response header to the supplied date-time formatted as an
822    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7231#section-7.1.1.1'>RFC 7231 IMF-fixdate</a>.
823    *
824    * <p>
825    * The supplied date-time is converted to GMT and truncated to whole seconds before formatting.
826    *
827    * @param value The last-modified timestamp. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
828    * @return This object.
829    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
830    */
831   public RestResponse lastModified(ZonedDateTime value) {
832      assertArgNotNull(ARG_VALUE, value);
833      var z = value.withZoneSameInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC).truncatedTo(SECONDS);
834      return setHeader(LastModified.of(RFC_1123_DATE_TIME.format(z)));
835   }
836
837   /**
838    * Sets the <c>Cache-Control</c> response header to the supplied directive string.
839    *
840    * <p>
841    * The value is written verbatim — the caller is responsible for supplying a comma-separated
842    * directive list per <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9111#name-cache-control'>RFC
843    * 9111 §5.2</a>.
844    *
845    * @param value The directive list (e.g. <js>"public, max-age=3600"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
846    * @return This object.
847    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
848    * @see #cacheControl(CacheControl.Builder)
849    */
850   public RestResponse cacheControl(String value) {
851      assertArgNotNull(ARG_VALUE, value);
852      return setHeader(CacheControl.of(value));
853   }
854
855   /**
856    * Sets the <c>Cache-Control</c> response header from a {@link org.apache.juneau.http.header.CacheControl.Builder}.
857    *
858    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
859    * <p class='bjava'>
860    *    <jv>res</jv>.cacheControl(CacheControl.<jsm>create</jsm>().publicCache().maxAge(3600));
861    *    <jc>// Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600</jc>
862    * </p>
863    *
864    * @param value The builder. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
865    * @return This object.
866    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
867    */
868   public RestResponse cacheControl(CacheControl.Builder value) {
869      assertArgNotNull(ARG_VALUE, value);
870      return cacheControl(value.build());
871   }
872
873   /**
874    * Sets a header on the request.
875    *
876    * @param schema
877    *    The schema to use to serialize the header, or <jk>null</jk> to use the default schema.
878    * @param name The header name.
879    * @param value The header value.
880    *    <ul>
881    *       <li>Can be any POJO.
882    *       <li>Converted to a string using the specified part serializer.
883    *    </ul>
884    * @return This object.
885    * @throws SchemaValidationException Header failed schema validation.
886    * @throws SerializeException Header could not be serialized.
887    */
888   public RestResponse setHeader(HttpPartSchema schema, String name, Object value) throws SchemaValidationException, SerializeException {
889      setHeader(name, request.getPartSerializerSession().serialize(HEADER, schema, value));
890      return this;
891   }
892
893   /**
894    * Sets a header on the request.
895    *
896    * @param name The header name.
897    * @param value The header value.
898    *    <ul>
899    *       <li>Can be any POJO.
900    *       <li>Converted to a string using the specified part serializer.
901    *    </ul>
902    * @return This object.
903    * @throws SchemaValidationException Header failed schema validation.
904    * @throws SerializeException Header could not be serialized.
905    */
906   public RestResponse setHeader(String name, Object value) throws SchemaValidationException, SerializeException {
907      setHeader(name, request.getPartSerializerSession().serialize(HEADER, null, value));
908      return this;
909   }
910
911   /**
912    * Sets a response header with the given name and value.
913    *
914    * <p>
915    * If the header had already been set, the new value overwrites the previous one.
916    *
917    * <p>
918    * The {@link #containsHeader(String)} method can be used to test for the presence of a header before setting its value.
919    *
920    * @param name The header name.
921    * @param value The header value.
922    */
923   @Override
924   public void setHeader(String name, String value) {
925
926      // Jetty doesn't set the content type correctly if set through this method.
927      // Tomcat/WAS does.
928      if (eqic(name, HEADER_ContentType)) {
929         inner.setContentType(value);
930         ContentType ct = ContentType.of(value);
931         if (nn(ct) && nn(ct.getParameter("charset")))
932            inner.setCharacterEncoding(ct.getParameter("charset"));
933      } else {
934         if (safeHeaders)
935            value = stripInvalidHttpHeaderChars(value);
936         value = abbreviate(value, maxHeaderLength);
937         inner.setHeader(name, value);
938      }
939   }
940
941   /**
942    * Specifies the maximum length for header values.
943    *
944    * <p>
945    * Header values that exceed this length will get truncated.
946    *
947    * @param value The new value for this setting.  The default is <c>8096</c>.
948    * @return This object.
949    */
950   public RestResponse setMaxHeaderLength(int value) {
951      maxHeaderLength = value;
952      return this;
953   }
954
955   /**
956    * Shortcut for calling <c>setNoTrace(<jk>true</jk>)</c>.
957    *
958    * @return This object.
959    */
960   public RestResponse setNoTrace() {
961      return setNoTrace(true);
962   }
963
964   /**
965    * Sets the <js>"NoTrace"</js> attribute to the specified boolean.
966    *
967    * <p>
968    * This flag is used by {@link CallLogger} and tells it not to log the current request.
969    *
970    * @param b The attribute value.
971    * @return This object.
972    */
973   public RestResponse setNoTrace(Boolean b) {
974      request.setNoTrace(b);
975      return this;
976   }
977
978   /**
979    * Sets metadata about this response.
980    *
981    * @param rbm The metadata about this response.
982    * @return This object.
983    */
984   public RestResponse setResponseBeanMeta(ResponseBeanMeta rbm) {
985      this.responseBeanMeta = rbm;
986      return this;
987   }
988
989   /**
990    * Enabled safe-header mode.
991    *
992    * <p>
993    * When enabled, invalid characters such as CTRL characters will be stripped from header values
994    * before they get set.
995    *
996    * @return This object.
997    */
998   public RestResponse setSafeHeaders() {
999      this.safeHeaders = true;
1000      return this;
1001   }
1002
1003   @SuppressWarnings({
1004      "java:S2789" // null check on Optional is intentional - content field can be null if never set
1005   })
1006   private Object getRawOutput() { return content == null ? null : content.orElse(null); }
1007
1008   private FinishablePrintWriter getWriter(boolean raw, boolean autoflush) throws NotAcceptable, IOException {
1009      if (nn(w))
1010         return w;
1011
1012      // If plain text requested, override it now.
1013      if (request.isPlainText())
1014         setHeader(HEADER_ContentType, "text/plain");
1015
1016      try {
1017         OutputStream out = (raw ? getOutputStream() : getNegotiatedOutputStream());
1018         w = new FinishablePrintWriter(out, getCharacterEncoding(), autoflush);
1019         return w;
1020      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") UnsupportedEncodingException e) {
1021         var ce = getCharacterEncoding();
1022         setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8");
1023         throw new NotAcceptable("Unsupported charset in request header ''Accept-Charset'': ''{0}''", ce);
1024      }
1025   }
1026
1027   private String resolveUris(Object value) {
1028      var s = s(value);
1029      return request.getUriResolver().resolve(s);
1030   }
1031
1032   private String maybeResolveUris(String name, Object value) {
1033      return isUriBearingHeader(name) ? resolveUris(value) : s(value);
1034   }
1035
1036   /**
1037    * If a (Content-Security-Policy) header value contains a nonce placeholder, mints a single per-response nonce
1038    * (cached as a request attribute so the serializer stamps the SAME value onto inline {@code <script>}/{@code <style>}
1039    * tags) and substitutes it into the value.  Returns the value unchanged when no placeholder is present, so this is
1040    * a no-op for every non-CSP header and for CSP headers that don't use a nonce.
1041    *
1042    * @param value The already-URI-resolved header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
1043    * @return The header value with any nonce placeholder resolved.
1044    */
1045   private String resolveCspNonce(String value) {
1046      if (value == null || ! value.contains(ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder.NONCE_PLACEHOLDER))
1047         return value;
1048      var nonce = request.getAttribute(RestRequest.CSP_NONCE_ATTR).as(String.class).orElse(null);
1049      if (nonce == null) {
1050         nonce = ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder.generateNonce();
1051         request.setAttribute(RestRequest.CSP_NONCE_ATTR, nonce);
1052      }
1053      return ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder.resolveNonce(value, nonce);
1054   }
1055
1056   private static boolean isUriBearingHeader(String name) {
1057      if (name == null)
1058         return false;
1059      return name.equalsIgnoreCase("Location")
1060         || name.equalsIgnoreCase("Content-Location")
1061         || name.equalsIgnoreCase("Referer");
1062   }
1063}
